perturbation.original_text: That you, shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidential a nd not disclose to any third party or make any use of the Confident ial Information beyond those ac tivities that are part of the Focus Group.
perturbation.changed_text: That you, shall at all times hold in trust and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the Confident ial Information beyond those ac tivities that are part of the Focus Group.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'keep confidential' from this clause, the participant is no longer explicitly obligated to maintain the confidentiality of the information. This removal contradicts the overall intent of the agreement. Although it obligates them to 'hold in trust', the absence of a direct requirement to 'keep confidential' introduces ambiguity, especially given prior definition changes, thus there is an in-text contradiction because the entire agreement is about being confidential and here that is removed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Also included as confidential is any participants Personally Id entifiable Information (“PII”). PII shall mean a person’s identity or information that might re asonably allow identification of the person. I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidenti al and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the participant must 'hold in trust, keep confidential', while the changed text removes the obligation to 'keep confidential'. The contradicted text states 'I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidential'. By removing 'keep confidential' from the changed text, the participant is no longer explicitly obligated to maintain the confidentiality of the information. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which explicitly requires confidentiality. This is in line with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition as essential language was removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Also included as confidential is any participants Personally Id entifiable Information (“PII”). PII shall mean a person’s identity or information that might re asonably allow identification of the person. I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidenti al and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.changed_text: Also included is any participants Personally Identifiable Information (“PII”). PII shall mean a person’s identity or information that might reasonably allow identification of the person. I shall at all times hold in trust and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'as confidential' and 'keep confidential' related to PII, the clause no longer explicitly designates the PII of participants as confidential, contradicting the overall intention of a confidentiality agreement. While the participant is obligated to 'hold in trust' the PII, the specific duty to maintain its confidentiality is now missing, which makes the agreement weaker and creates an in-text contradiction with the introduction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Thank you for agreeing to participate in a focus group to discuss new products. The products themselves, and the ideas and concepts regarding its manufacture, marketing, use, and the discussions here represent Information of the focus group. I, ____________________________________________________________ ___ hereby agree t o maintain the confidentiality of information disclosed during focus group or observed live as follows:
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that PII is 'as confidential' and that the participant must 'keep confidenti al' this information. The changed text removes this explicit confidentiality obligation. The contradicted text emphasizes the agreement to 'maintain the confidentiality of information disclosed during focus group'. By removing the explicit confidentiality requirement for PII in the changed text, it creates a contradiction because the participant is no longer explicitly agreeing to keep PII confidential, conflicting with the overall confidentiality agreement outlined in the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition as essential language was removed from one section which contradicts obligations in the other.

perturbation.original_text: By submitting this form you will be entering a Non‐Disclosure a greement with: Kenall Manufacturing, Co., 10200 55 th Street Kenosha, WI 53144
perturbation.changed_text: By submitting this form you will be entering an agreement with: Kenall Manufacturing, Co., 10200 55 th Street Kenosha, WI 53144
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Non-Disclosure Agreement' to simply 'agreement' undermines the core purpose of the document. The absence of 'Non-Disclosure' creates a contradiction, weakening the agreement's explicit commitment to confidentiality. This directly contradicts previous clauses that operate under the assumption that the agreement is, in fact, a Non-Disclosure Agreement. While implied, it creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To hold any and all technical or business information about the company’s product which is disclosed, or made available to you directly or indirectly, or is information you otherwise receive incident to your participation in this discussion
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated 'Non-Disclosure Agreement,' creating a clear expectation of confidentiality. The changed text removes 'Non-Disclosure,' weakening this commitment and introducing uncertainty. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes the agreement is a Non-Disclosure Agreement focused on protecting 'technical or business information.' Without the explicit 'Non-Disclosure' aspect, the agreement's enforceability regarding confidential information is compromised, causing a strong in-text contradiction as it no longer explicitly ensures the protection described in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: That you acknowledge yo u are being sufficiently compensated for your participation in this Focus Group and that all informat ion and opinions you provide a re solely your own and are in no way reflective of your employer(s).
perturbation.changed_text: That you acknowledge you are being compensated for your participation in this Focus Group and that all information and opinions you provide are solely your own and are in no way reflective of your employer(s).
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'sufficiently' suggests the compensation may not be adequate, potentially weakening the consideration element of the contract. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the respondent may later argue the compensation wasn't 'sufficient' consideration for the broad confidentiality obligations. It's now undefined whether the consideration is substantial enough, potentially invalidating the agreement if a participant later argues that the compensation was inadequate, thus the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Thank you for agreeing to participate in a focus group to discuss new products. The products themselves, and the ideas and concepts regarding its manufacture, marketing, use, and the discussions here represent Information of the focus group. I, ____________________________________________________________ ___ hereby agree t o maintain the confidentiality of information disclosed during focus group or observed live as follows:
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'sufficiently compensated,' implying a clear and agreed-upon value. The changed text removes 'sufficiently,' creating ambiguity about whether the compensation is adequate. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which outlines a confidentiality agreement based on participation in the focus group. If the compensation is now potentially insufficient, participants could argue the confidentiality agreement is not binding due to lack of adequate consideration, creating an in-text contradiction and potential unenforceability.
